etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "laid" + "back" from Middle English "leyen" + "bæc" (placed backward)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Placed backward; having relaxed easygoing and laid back relaxed with easygoing presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Placed backward → relaxed easygoing → contemporary laid back relaxed and laid back easygoing with relaxed presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Placed backward → relaxed easygoing → laid back relaxed → relaxed understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having laid back relaxed and laid back easygoing; experiencing relaxed easygoing and laid back laid-backness with laid back presence
contemporary_usage.colloquial_usage: Usage**: "Laid back person," "laid back attitude," "being laid back"
contemporary_usage.register: Positive; indicates relaxation and easygoing calm with casual laid-backness
